Confirmation Messages: Send Out Confirmation Messages For Existing Users Opting In
Description:
Confirmation messages are sent out for users who have previously consented to being contacted by MiCourt Reminders. In the event that one of these users is being added to a payment plan, one of these confirmation messages should be sent out instead of a consent message.

The existing functionality for sending consent messages should be nearly the same for this effort.

Reminders API
Build a new endpoint for sending out these emails and texts messages.
Acceptance Criteria: For users that have already been marked as consented, they receive the confirmation message/email instead of the prompt for consent.

Title: Finance Release 6-4-24
Consent Messages: Send out Consent Messages for Finance Reminders
Description:
A new consent message must be sent out when a reminder is added to a payment plan and the user has not already responded to a consent message for the given contact.

Reminders API
Create a POST endpoint that sends the consent messages out. It should take a list of reminders and send them out via the appropriate messaging service. The endpoint should return a count of how many consent messages were successfully sent out.
Acceptance Criteria:
• Text reminders should be sent out with Twilio
  • Email reminders should be sent out with SendGrid
  • Both can be viewed and tested in real time (i.e. we can receive a consent message)

Title: GET endpoint for sending upcoming reminders to the Reminders API
Description:
In the Finance API, create a new endpoint that returns a list of reminders that will be sent to the reminders api, who will then prepare to send them out to users.

A reminder is considered upcoming if:
  • its payment plan's due date is within 5 days of the current date
  • consent has been granted for the given tenant
The shape of a reminder should be:
  • tenant key
  • contact information
  • payment due date
  • payment amount
Acceptance Criteria: • Get endpoint returns all upcoming reminders that need to be sent out in the proper shape

Title: Consent Messages: Receiving a Consent Response for Emails
Description:
When a user responds to their consent email (they click the blue confirm button), we need to update the consent status of the reminder associated with that contact value.

This is already implemented for text messages, but emails in the reminders api are created/stored with a unique consent token. This guid is used for the `v2/reminders/subscribe` and `v2/reminders/unsubscribe` PUT endpoints, allowing for the email's consent status to be updated accordingly. In finance, we do not have any concept of this token.

We'll create a concept of this consent token for finance, and it will be created only when email reminder (whose consent token does not exist in reminders already) is added to the system.

Finance API
Add a new field for the email consent token to the relevant models for reminders. We'll also need to update the methods used to create reminders in the Create/Edit payment plan workflows.

We may also need a new GET endpoint that retrieves this guid for the reminders api, but it's possible we can modify the `VerifyContactValueAsync` GET endpoint to serve this purpose.

Reminders API
Implement the usage of this consent token in the `BuildConsentMessage` method in the `EmailAccountService.cs`. We need to leave the existing functionality intact, but add support for reminders that exist in our finance system.
Acceptance Criteria:
• when a user responds to an email consent message, they are redirected to the correct micourt page appropriately indicating their decision
  • the corresponding reminder's consent status is updated in the finance db

Title: Consent Messages: Update Consent Status of a Reminder
Description:
After a user responds to their consent message, we need to update the corresponding reminder's consent status with their response.

Finance API
Create a PUT endpoint that updates the consent status value for all reminders in the Finance DB matching the contact type, contact value and court tenant.
Acceptance Criteria: • the consent status of existing reminders can be updated to a new value

Title: Consent Messages: GET Reminders from Reminders DB
Description:
When we add a new reminder to a payment plan, it gets created and stored in the finance database. For its contact value (email or phone), we need to check the Reminders DB and see if it has already been granted consent for the court tenant of the payment plan.

Reminders API
Create a GET Endpoint that returns all reminders from the Reminders DB that match a given contact value and type. The endpoint should also send back the consent status of the reminder retrieved.

We'll hit this endpoint from finance.
Acceptance Criteria:
• endpoint returns a list of reminders found in the Reminders DB that match the contact value and court tenant params
  • if no reminders are found, return an empty list

Title: Modify Edit Payment Plan Endpoint to Include Editing Existing Reminders
Description:
In Finance API:

Reminders need to be included in the response for the edit payment plan endpoint. That way when we go to edit/save a modified payment plan, it also will save the reminders if they were modified as well.
Acceptance Criteria:
• Edit Payment Plan endpoint should contain reminders in the request body
  • upon successfully editing a payment plan:
    • changes to reminders should be reflected in the database
    • if there were no changes to the reminders, there should be no db transactions in regards to the reminders table

Title: Modify the Add Payment Plan Endpoint to Include Adding New Reminders
Description:
In the Finance API:

Reminders need to be included in the response for the add payment plan endpoint. That way when we go to save the payment plan, it also will save the reminders that a user has added.
Acceptance Criteria:
• Add Payment Plan endpoint should contain reminders in the request body
  • upon successfully adding a payment plan, the reminders should be stored in their respective database table with the correct information from the request body

Title: Set Up Reminders in Finance API
Description:
This story is just the initial set up for us to do future Reminders work in the finance API.

We need to set up the reminders table and update relevant Payment Plan Repository functions & dto's to include reminders.

It's likely that unit/acceptance tests will also be need to be modified.
Acceptance Criteria:
• Reminders should be added to all Payment Plan responses
  • Reminders table should be created and contain the following fields
    • Id
    • Payment Plan ID (FK)
    • Contact Type
    • Contact Value
    • Consent Granted
  • Link to the schema

Title: JIS Admin is warned when trying to leave Form Template creation page without submitting
Description:
Prompt user with a modal to confirm their intent to cancel the creation of a form template version if they have not submitted it.  

Use case: JIS Admin creates a new template, or a new template version, then starts the creation of the template and clicks the close button in the navbar.  A modal would ask them if they want to continue or go back and save the work they have done.  This is to help prevent losing the mapping work, which could take users a decent amount of time to complete.

This should work for navigation events such as typing a new route in the address bar, or UI events like clicking the close button in the upper left of the component or the Cancel button in the upper right.

https://www.figma.com/file/f577nrKafqKB7x3rvRHMOt/JIS-MiCourt?node-id=2871%3A39080

Dev Note: You may want to use a De-Activate Route Guard: https://angular.io/api/router/CanDeactivate
Acceptance Criteria:
If the user has started the process of creating a new form template or form template version they are prompted by a modal before navigating away.

Confirm design and language of the prompt with Ian and Bryan.

  • On failure, the modal remains open and displays an error state

  • If the forms API call fails, a toast message should signal this failure to the user

Title: Show a relevant error message when the form template creation submission fails
Description:
Currently, a static error message will be displayed when the create form template submission fails. We can instead display a more useful error message to the user when this happens, i.e., "Error: a form with that name already exists".

The API returns the message of the error when there is a bad request, which is usually thrown when there's a problem with validation of the request, including for a duplicate form name. The message in this exception can be used in a snackbar message when the error is handled in the UI so that the user sees all errors there may have been in their request. (This shouldn't happen too much in practice, assuming our input field validation when creating new form templates works as we expect)

Here is where the currently static error message gets set.
Acceptance Criteria:
• The user sees a more descriptive error message when create form template submission fails
    • This error message should be directly taken from the API response returned as part of the ProblemDetails object

  • Update the following FormRepository functions to include working with the IsActive column: AddNewFormTemplateAsync, AddNewFormTemplateVersionAsync, UpdateFormTemplateVersionAsync.

Title: Add a forms UI route for displaying DCS forms
Description:
In WebDCS, the case type is often not visible on the screen to be able to make a complete docket-Id for Forms API requests. The #30773 added a new API endpoint for downloading mapped forms that uses the division (Civil or Traffic/Criminal) to get a full docket-id from the case search API.

Create a new route in the Forms-UI project that will be used for WebTCS downloads. It should operate similarly to the `/display-legacy` route. 
display-legacy-dcs/:legacyKey/:division/:caseYear/:caseNumber/:courtKey/:formNumber

Title: Case data is mapped for cc219b
Export Forms library from Forms UI project
Description:
The `@jis/forms` library project in the MiCourt.Forms.Ui repository needs to be configured to be built into an npm package that can be consumed by SuiteAdmin and other JIS applications.
  • A pipeline needs to be configured for building the library
  • A deployment pipeline needs to be configured for publishing the library artifact to the JIS npm repository
A good reference for this would be the `@jis/manage-reminders` library which is part of the Reminders UI project.
Acceptance Criteria: Whenever the `@jis/forms` project has changes pushed to the master branch, a pipeline should run to build the library, and then publish the artifact to to be made available in the JIS Artifacts Feed.

Implement Party Records Search Results Table in Case Search UI
Description:
In the party records tab of the Case Search UI, we need to implement the design and functionality of the search results table.
  • The results table should match the designs as seen in Figma
    • The first column should be fixed width
    • The first column should include a checkbox that is toggled when clicked directly, or when the entire row is clicked
    • The remaining columns should display the entire content of their cells, and if this makes the table too wide for the browser window, the table should be able to scroll horizontally
    • This story does not include the hyperlink for case numbers in the results table
    • The table should be paginated client side
  • The Batch Changes button should be added on the right side above the search results table
    • When there are one or more rows selected in the results table, the Batch Changes button should be enabled
    • The batch changes button does not need to be made functional as part of this story

Links:
Material Design Tables with sticky columns
Acceptance Criteria:
• Results table matches designs from Figma
  • First column is fixed width, and has a checkbox preceding the Party Name
  • Remaining columns show complete information, and when this lengthens the table too much horizontally, the table should be scrollable horizontally
  • Rows can be clicked on to toggle their selection, and the checkbox of each row indicates whether it's selected
  • When one or more rows are selected, the Batch Changes button is enabled
    • No functionality for the button is necessary as part of this story, just toggling that it is disabled or not

Title: Implement Party Records Search page in Case Search UI
Description:
In the party records tab of the Case Search UI, implement the following pieces of functionality:

  • The results table does not need to fit the designs as shown, it only needs to be capable of displaying party records search results, which include the columns seen in the designs below
    • For this story, it doesn't need to actually get that information from the API yet, just to read data from where search results would be stored
  • This story does not include adding the Batch Changes button in the upper right of the results
  • This story does not include the "More Options" button below the search inputs
Acceptance Criteria:
• Add a search header with a tenant selector and name input, that when enter is pressed or the magnifying glass is clicked, triggers a call to a service to search for party records
    • Actual requests to the API not necessary for this story
  • Add a basic results table to the body of the page that can show results from a party records search
    • At a minimum include columns for Party, date of birth, drivers license, case number, address, email, mobile phone, and home phone
